tool_args.label: Utica Avenue (B46) bus corridor

payload.description: Utica Avenue (B46) bus corridor was identified as a potential candidate for Select Bus Service. carries nearly 50,000 passengers per day, the second-busiest bus route in NYC.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Utica Avenue (B46) bus corridor was identified as a potential candidate for Select Bus Service in the "Bus Rapid Transit: Phase II Study" (2009).

tool_args.label: Utica Av corridor from St. John's Pl to Church Av

payload.limits: from St. John's Pl to Church Av

evidence_refs.source_quote: Offset and curbside bus lanes on 1.3 miles of Utica Av corridor from St. John's Pl to Church Av

tool_args.label: Utica Avenue Select Bus Service

payload.description: Build on existing Bus Priority and Safety Project to upgrade B46 Limited to B46 SBS. Includes off-board fare collection, additional bus lanes, transit signal priority, service planning changes, and capital improvements at SBS stations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Build on existing Bus Priority and Safety Project to upgrade B46 Limited to B46 SBS

tool_args.label: 2014 Bus Priority and Safety Improvement Project - Utica Avenue

payload.project_name: 2014 Bus Priority and Safety Improvement Project
payload.description: Transit improvements including offset and curbside bus lanes on 1.3 miles of Utica Av corridor from St. John's Pl to Church Av. Traffic and safety improvements including left-turn restrictions and left-turn bay additions. Parking changes: +19 parking spaces near Eastern Parkway, -22 parking spaces along southern part of corridor, new loading zones.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Offset and curbside bus lanes on 1.3 miles of Utica Av corridor from St. John's Pl to Church Av
